appBearerAuth.description: Use `Authorization: Bearer <APP_ACCESS_TOKEN>`. This is the access token returned by POST /api/v1/auth/session or POST /api/v1/auth/refresh, and is used for protected API calls.

firebase_id_token.description: Firebase ID token from the Firebase client SDK (sent in the request body for session bootstrap).

role.description: Server-managed role (defaults to student). Clients cannot set this value.

session_id.description: Session identifier (also used as the refresh token). Store this if you plan to refresh or to logout with a Firebase token (via x-session-id).

access_token.description: App access token. Use this as `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` for protected API calls.

refresh_token.description: Refresh token (same value as session.session_id). Send this in POST /api/v1/auth/refresh.

plan.description: Current plan/package code (e.g. free, pro, or an admin-created package code).

documentId.description: Legacy alias of document_id (accepted for backwards compatibility).

types.description: Requested study set types. Duplicates are ignored. Legacy alias: quiz → multiple_choice.

estimated_credits.description: Total credits reserved up-front for the whole batch.

url.description: WebSocket URL to subscribe to batch progress updates.

token.description: Short-lived token used to authenticate the WebSocket connection. Send it as a JSON message: {"type":"auth","token":"..."}.

model_name.description: Model used to generate this output (if available).

provider.description: AI provider used to generate this output (if available).

schema.description: Filter by plan/package code (e.g. free, pro).

post.summary: Bootstrap auth session (register-or-login)
post.description: Single entry auth endpoint. Send `firebase_id_token` in the JSON body (a Firebase ID token from the Firebase client SDK). Backend verifies the token using Firebase Admin SDK, syncs profile from Firebase, creates user/subscription if first login, and returns an app access token plus refresh/session token. Use the returned `auth.access_token` as the Bearer token for other protected API calls.

201.description: New user auto-registered and session created.

delete.description: Logs out the current app session by revoking the session/refresh token (session.session_id). This also immediately invalidates any app access tokens issued for that session (they are checked against the active session in the database). Authenticate using the app access token (from `auth.access_token`).

post.summary: Refresh app access token
post.description: No Authorization header required. Provide `refresh_token` (same value as `session.session_id`) to rotate the refresh/session and receive a new `auth.access_token` plus new `session.session_id`.

Create a Stripe Checkout session for a subscription plan
Purchase a one-off credit pack (Stripe Checkout)

topup_pack_id.description: Credit pack identifier (use the pack `id` (UUID) or `code` returned by GET /api/v1/payments/packs).

post.summary: Generate study set materials (notes, flashcards, multiple choice, etc.)
post.description:
Creates a batch and enqueues one job per requested type (duplicates are ignored). Jobs run once by default (no automatic retries).

Progress updates: connect to the returned websocket.url and send `{"type":"auth","token":"<token>"}`. After auth, you will receive JSON events such as:
- `{"type":"connected","batch_id":"...","subscribed_at":"..."}`
- `{"type":"job_started","job_id":"...","task_type":"notes","timestamp":"..."}`
- `{"type":"job_completed","job_id":"...","task_type":"notes","output_id":"...","credits_consumed":123,"timestamp":"..."}`
- `{"type":"job_failed","job_id":"...","task_type":"notes","error":"...","credits_released":123,"timestamp":"..."}`
- `{"type":"batch_completed","batch_id":"...","total_jobs":3,"completed_jobs":2,"failed_jobs":1,"total_credits_consumed":234,"timestamp":"..."}`

202.description: Batch created, jobs enqueued, websocket token returned.

get.description: Returns the completed notes output as Markdown. If the output is not completed yet, returns a 422 validation error.

Returns a Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream that replays the stored Markdown notes in chunks.

Events:
- metadata: JSON object describing the output (output_id, type, document_id, model_name, provider)
- delta: JSON object `{ "delta": "..." }` (chunk of markdown)
- done: JSON object describing usage and credits consumed

Note: if the output is not completed yet, the server returns a normal JSON error response (422) and does not start an SSE stream.
Returns completed flashcards. If the output is not completed yet, returns 422.
Returns completed multiple choice questions. If the output is not completed yet, returns 422.

get.summary: Get generated fill in blanks output
get.description: Returns completed fill-in-the-blank questions. If the output is not completed yet, returns 422.
Returns completed written test questions. If the output is not completed yet, returns 422.
Returns completed tutor lesson content. If the output is not completed yet, returns 422.
Returns completed podcast script payload. If the output is not completed yet, returns 422.
Returns the completed study guide as Markdown. If the output is not completed yet, returns 422.
